title: 10 best herbal creams for pink eye

remedy_properties: Calendula's anti-inflammatory property: Calendula helps reduce inflammation and swelling in the affected area, which alleviates discomfort and redness associated with pink eye.

Antioxidant and antimicrobial properties: Calendula's antioxidants neutralize free radicals that can exacerbate pink eye symptoms, while its antimicrobial properties help combat bacterial or viral infections causing the condition.

Soften and lubricate the cornea property: Calendula's emollient and soothing properties help soften and lubricate the affected area, promoting healing and reducing irritation by creating a protective barrier against further irritation.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis, also known as pot marigold, creams helps with pink eye because of its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The cream's active compounds, such as triterpenoids and carotenoids, help to soothe and calm the affected eye, reducing redness and swelling. Additionally, its natural antiseptic properties combat bacterial and viral infections that can cause pink eye, promoting a faster recovery and reducing the risk of complications. This natural remedy provides gentle, effective relief for pink eye sufferers.
remedy_constituents: Triterpenoids: They help reduce inflammation and promote healing in the affected area, alleviating symptoms of pink eye.
Flavonoids: They possess antimicrobial properties, which inhibit the growth of bacteria and viruses that cause pink eye, thus preventing infection and promoting recovery.

Nascent sesquiterpene lactones (such as calendulic acid): They have anti-inflammatory and immune-boosting effects, which help to reduce the severity of pink eye symptoms and accelerate the healing process.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers of Calendula officinalis are used to make creams for pink eye due to their anti-inflammatory properties, which help soothe and calm irritated conjunctiva.
Seeds: The seeds of Calendula officinalis are used in creams for pink eye because they contain triterpenoids, which have antimicrobial properties that aid in reducing bacterial and viral infections.
Leaves: The leaves of Calendula officinalis are used in creams for pink eye due to their ability to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing, which helps alleviate symptoms of pink eye.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of dried calendula officinalis flowers from a trusted supplier or your own garden after 2 weeks of blooming.
Steep 2 cups of dried flowers in 4 cups of boiling water for 5-7 minutes to create a strong infusion.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean bowl to remove solids.
Mix 1 cup of beeswax with 1 cup of shea butter and 1/2 cup of coconut oil in a double boiler at 160-180°f.
Combine the infused liquid with 1 cup of distilled water and the cooled wax mixture to create the cream base.

remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Purple coneflower contains compounds like triterpenoids and sesquiterpenoids, which have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria and viruses, helping to combat infections and reduce inflammation in pink eye.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The cream's active ingredients, such as apigenin and luteolin, have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help soothe redness, swelling, and discharge associated with pink eye.

Immune-boosting properties: Purple coneflower has been traditionally used to support the immune system, and its compounds may help stimulate the body's natural defense against infections, such as pink eye.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea, also known as purple coneflower, creams helps with pink eye because of its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The cream's active compounds, such as alkylamides and caffeic acid, help to reduce inflammation and combat bacterial and viral infections that cause pink eye. Additionally, Echinacea's immunomodulatory effects support the body's natural defense mechanisms, promoting a speedy recovery and reducing the severity of symptoms. This natural remedy provides relief from pink eye's discomfort and promotes overall eye health.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid Glycosides: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce the swelling and discomfort associated with pink eye.

Flavonoids (including Quercetin): Quercetin, a type of flavonoid present in Echinacea, has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help alleviate pink eye symptoms such as redness and itching.

Triterpene Saponins (including Alkylamides): These compounds have antimicrobial properties, which can help combat bacterial and viral infections that cause pink eye, promoting the healing process and reducing the risk of complications.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making them effective in soothing and protecting the affected area.
Roots: They are rich in echinacosides, which have been shown to have immunomodulatory effects, helping to reduce inflammation and promote healing.
Leaves: They have a high concentration of caffeic acid derivatives, which possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties that can help combat pink eye infections.
remedy_recipe: Harvest echinacea purpurea roots in late summer or early fall after the plant has finished blooming.
Dry the roots in a low-temperature oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 2-3 hours.
Grind 2 tablespoons of dried root into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Mix the powder with 2 ounces of distilled water to create a strong infusion.
Combine 1 tablespoon of beeswax and 1 tablespoon of shea butter with the infusion in a double boiler.

remedy_properties: Soothing and anti-inflammatory: Aloe vera's unique combination of vitamins, minerals, and amino acids helps to reduce redness, swelling, and discomfort associated with pink eye, allowing for faster recovery.

Antimicrobial and antiviral: The anthraquinones present in aloe vera have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria and viruses that cause pink eye, promoting the healing process and preventing further infection.

Wound-healing and regenerative: Aloe vera's polysaccharides help to stimulate collagen production, promote tissue repair, and enhance the body's natural defense mechanisms, allowing for faster recovery and reduced scarring.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe vera, also known as aloe, creams helps with pink eye because of its soothing and anti-inflammatory properties. The gel from the aloe vera plant contains compounds that reduce redness and swelling, providing relief from the discomfort associated with pink eye. Aloe vera's antimicrobial properties also help combat the bacterial or viral infection causing the condition, while its moisturizing properties soothe and calm the affected area, promoting quick healing and reducing the risk of complications. This natural remedy offers a gentle and effective solution for pink eye sufferers.
remedy_constituents: Aloe-emodin: Aloe-emodin is a anthraquinone compound that helps reduce inflammation and prevent bacterial growth, thereby alleviating the symptoms of pink eye.
Saponins: Saponins are a type of triterpenoid glycoside that exhibit anti-inflammatory properties, which help soothe the affected eyes and reduce discomfort associated with pink eye.
Vitamins A and E: Vitamins A and E are potent antioxidants present in Aloe vera that help protect the eyes from oxidative stress, promote healing, and reduce the risk of further infection.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Aloe vera leaves are the primary source of the gel and juice used in creams to treat pink eye due to their high content of anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
Seeds: Aloe vera seeds are used to extract compounds like aloin and aloe-emodin, which have antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in treating pink eye.
Barks: Aloe vera barks contain a high amount of aloin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are used to treat pink eye and other eye infections.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh aloe vera gel from mature plants and rinse it with distilled water.
Combine the aloe vera gel with 50g of beeswax and 50g of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Add 20g of shea butter and 10g of vitamin e oil to the mixture and stir until melted.
Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool for 30 minutes before whipping it.
Transfer the whipped cream into a glass jar and store it in the refrigerator for up to 6 months.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Herbal licorice cream contains anti-inflammatory compounds such as glycyrrhizin and liquiritin, which help reduce redness, swelling, and discomfort associated with pink eye.

Antibacterial and antiviral properties: Licorice root has been shown to exhibit antibacterial and antiviral activities against various microorganisms, including those that can cause conjunctivitis (pink eye), helping to combat the infection.

Soften and lubricate the eyes: The demulcent properties of licorice cream help soothe and moisturize the affected area, reducing dryness, itchiness, and discomfort, making it an effective topical treatment for pink eye.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra, also known as licorice, creams helps with pink eye because of its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. These compounds also help combat bacterial and viral infections that cause pink eye, promoting faster recovery. remedy_constituents: Saponins: These triterpene saponins help with pink eye by reducing inflammation and combating bacterial infections, which are common causes of pink eye.
Glycyrrhizin: This triterpene glycoside has anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate symptoms of pink eye, such as redness and swelling, and also exhibits antimicrobial activity.

Flavonoids: The flavonoids present in Glycyrrhiza glabra creams, particularly luteolin and kaempferol, possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help protect the eye from oxidative stress and inflammation associated with pink eye.
remedy_parts: Roots: Roots are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of glycyrrhizin, which has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and discomfort associated with pink eye.
Leaves: Leaves are used for their soothing and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to calm and protect the affected eye.
Barks: Barks are used for their astringent and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce redness and swelling in the eye.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots that have been stored in an airtight container for at least 6 months.
Combine 100g of the dried roots with 200ml of distilled water in a saucepan and bring to a boil.
Reduce heat to low and simmer for 30-40 minutes or until the mixture has reduced to 50% of its original volume.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container and discard the solids.
Mix 10g of beeswax with 20g of coconut oil and 20g of shea butter in a double boiler to create a smooth cream.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Dandelion's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as taraxasterol and triterpenoids, reduce redness, swelling, and irritation associated with pink eye.

Antibacterial properties: The antimicrobial compounds in dandelion, including sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids, help combat bacterial infections that can cause pink eye, such as Haemophilus influenzae and Streptococcus pneumoniae.

Antioxidant properties: Dandelion's antioxidant compounds, like vitamins A, C, and E, neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress, which can contribute to the progression of pink eye and its associated symptoms.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Taraxacum officinale, also known as dandelion, creams helps with pink eye because of its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The cream's active compounds, such as taraxasterol and inulin, work to reduce swelling and combat bacterial and viral infections that cause pink eye. Apolactone: This phenolic compound has antioxidant properties, which can help soothe and calm the irritated conjunctiva and reduce the risk of further infection.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Their extracts are commonly used due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help soothe and treat pink eye.
Flowers: The dandelion flower extracts are often used for their antimicrobial properties to combat infections that cause pink eye.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers at peak potency during springtime.
Dry the taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers in a low-temperature oven at 30°c for 2 hours.
Infuse 20g of dried taraxacum officinale in 100ml of carrier oil at 60°c for 4 hours.
Mix 10ml of infused taraxacum officinale oil with 20g of beeswax and 20ml of distilled water at 60°c for 10 minutes.
Whip the mixture until it thickens and forms a smooth cream texture in 5 minutes.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Eyebright creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids, which help reduce redness and swelling associated with pink eye by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.

Antimicrobial agents: Herbal eyebright creams often contain antimicrobial agents like essential oils (e.g., tea tree oil) or herbs (e.g., calendula), which help kill bacteria, viruses, and fungi that cause pink eye, promoting healing and preventing further infection.

Flowers: The flowers contain flavonoids and anthocyanins, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that aid in soothing pink eye symptoms.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 500g of fresh euphrasia officinalis leaves in the early morning and clean them thoroughly with cold water.
Steep 200g of the cleaned leaves in 1000ml of hot distilled water for 30 minutes then strain and discard.
Combine 200g of beeswax with 300g of sweet almond oil in a double boiler and heat until melted.
Add 100g of the strained herbal infusion to the melted beeswax and oil mixture and stir well.
Pour the mixture into sterile containers and let it cool and set at room temperature for 30 minutes.

remedy_recipe: Harvest plantago major leaves in the morning after the dew has evaporated for optimal potency.
Dry the harvested leaves in a low-temperature oven at 105 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Combine 10 grams of dried plantago major leaves with 50 grams of base oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture in the double boiler at 160 degrees fahrenheit for 30 minutes to infuse the base oil.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth and bottle it as herbal cream for topical use.

remedy_recipe: Harvest a sufficient quantity of dried symphytum officinale root and leaves from a trusted supplier for 250 grams.
Combine 250 grams of dried symphytum officinale root and leaves with 500 milliliters of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil in a clean glass jar.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler for 6 to 8 hours to facilitate extraction of the active ingredients.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth and discard the solids then filter the liquid through a coffee filter paper for 30 minutes.
Mix 50 milliliters of the symphytum officinale extract with 50 grams of beeswax and 20 milliliters of vitamin e oil in a double boiler for 10 minutes.

Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container and discard the solids.
Mix 50g of beeswax with 50g of shea butter and 50g of coconut oil in a heat-resistant bowl.
Combine the infused oil with the melted beeswax mixture and stir until well combined and cooled slightly.

remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: St John's Wort cream helps reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the eye, which can exacerbate Pink Eye symptoms.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-20 grams of hypericum perforatum flowers and leaves at the peak of their potency when flowers are in full bloom.
Dry the harvested flowers and leaves in a low-temperature oven at 35-40°c for 2-3 hours to preserve their properties.
Combine the dried flowers and leaves with 100 grams of jojoba oil and 50 grams of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture in the double boiler at 60-70°c for 10-15 minutes while occasionally stirring until the beeswax melts completely.
Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool before transferring it to a glass jar for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Salad burnet creams helps with pink eye because of its anti-inflammatory properties. remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 ounces of dried sanguisorba minor leaves and stems.
Grind 1 ounce of the dried herb into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered herb with 8 ounces of distilled water in a glass bowl.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until it reaches a warm infusion.
Strain the infused liquid through cheesecloth into a clean glass jar, discarding the solids and allowing the cream to cool.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Blueberry creams helps with pink eye because of its unique combination of anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Herbal blueberry creams often contain antibacterial agents like salicylic acid or tea tree oil that help combat the bacterial infection causing pink eye (conjunctivitis). This helps reduce inflammation and promote healing.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The antioxidants and flavonoids present in herbal blueberry creams, such as anthocyanins, have anti-inflammatory effects that can help soothe and calm irritated eyes, reducing redness and swelling associated with pink eye.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of dried vaccinium myrtillus berries and grind them into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Combine the ground vaccinium myrtillus berries with 100ml of cold-pressed sweet almond oil in a double boiler, heating the mixture at 60°c for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container, discarding the solids and leaving behind the infused oil.
Mix the infused oil with 20g of beeswax and 10g of candelilla wax in a double boiler, melting the waxes at 70°c for 15 minutes.
Pour the melted wax mixture into sterilized glass jars and let it cool and set at room temperature for 2 hours before use.

remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry 50 grams of achyrocline satureioides leaves and flowers under shade for 7 days.
Grind the dried plant material into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Combine the powdered achyrocline satureioides with 100 grams of sweet almond oil in a double boiler at low heat for 30 minutes.
Filter the mixture through cheesecloth to remove any solids and allow it to cool to room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Horehound creams helps with pink eye because of its natural anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. remedy_recipe: Gather 50 grams of dried marrubium vulgare flowers and 20 grams of beeswax.
Combine the dried flowers with 100 milliliters of olive oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture at 60 degrees celsius for 30 minutes to infuse the flowers' properties.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth into a clean glass jar and discard solids.
Mix the strained oil with 10 grams of coconut oil and 5 grams of beeswax, heating until melted and blended.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian creams helps with pink eye because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The cream contains valerenic acid, a compound that has been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate itchiness associated with pink eye. Additionally, the moisturizing properties of the cream help to calm and soothe the affected area, promoting faster healing and reducing discomfort. By providing immediate relief from redness, itching, and burning sensations, herbal valerian creams can effectively treat mild to moderate cases of pink eye.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial and Anti-inflammatory Properties: Valerian cream's antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties help reduce inflammation, ease discomfort, and combat bacterial infections that cause pink eye.
Antioxidant Properties: Valerian's antioxidant properties help neutralize free radicals that can exacerbate the condition, reducing oxidative stress and promoting healthy tissue recovery.

Soothing and Calming Effects: Valerian's calming effects on the nervous system help reduce redness, irritation, and itching associated with pink eye, providing relief from discomfort and promoting a sense of calm.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in sesquiterpenes, which have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that help soothe and treat pink eye symptoms.
Rhyzomes: Contain a high concentration of valepotriates, which have antibacterial and antifungal effects, making them effective against pink eye-causing pathogens.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh valeriana officinalis roots and wash them thoroughly with distilled water.
Chop the cleaned roots into small pieces and then dry them in a warm oven at 150°f for 6 hours.
Steep 100g of dried valerian root in 500ml of boiling water for 20 minutes to create an infusion.
Combine the cooled infusion with 250g of beeswax, 125g of coconut oil, and 25g of shea butter, melting them together at low heat for 30 minutes.
Pour the mixture into sterilized containers and allow it to cool and solidify completely before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chinese peony creams helps with pink eye because of its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The peony plant, known for its medicinal value in traditional Chinese medicine, contains active compounds that effectively reduce redness and swelling associated with pink eye. When applied topically, the cream soothes and calms the affected area, promoting faster healing and reducing discomfort. Antiviral property: The herb Peony has antiviral activity, which helps to combat the underlying viral infection causing pink eye, reducing its severity and duration.
Soothing and lubricating property: The cream's moisturizing and soothing properties help to calm and protect the affected eye, promoting healing and reducing irritation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins, which help to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of pink eye.
Rhyzomes: Contain anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe redness and itching associated with pink eye.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh paeonia lactiflora flowers and leaves in the early morning or late evening to ensure optimal potency.
Dry the harvested paeonia lactiflora flowers and leaves in a well-ventilated area or using a food dehydrator at 30°c for 24 hours.
Combine 20 grams of dried paeonia lactiflora flowers, 10 grams of dried chamomile flowers, and 5 grams of beeswax pellets with 50 milliliters of sweet almond oil in a heat-resistant glass bowl.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler or a warm water bath at 60°c for 30 minutes to create a smooth and consistent texture.
Pour the hot herbal cream into sterilized glass jars, seal tightly, and store in the refrigerator at 4°c for up to six months or use within three weeks.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel creams helps with pink eye because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm irritated eyes. The herb's potent antioxidant compounds, such as flavonoids and terpenes, work to reduce swelling and discomfort associated with conjunctivitis. remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Fennel's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, help reduce redness and swelling associated with pink eye.
Antibacterial properties: The antibacterial compounds in fennel cream, including terpenes and volatile oils, work to combat the bacterial infection that causes pink eye.

remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, fennel roots help reduce redness and swelling associated with pink eye.
Seeds: Containing omega-3 fatty acids and other essential oils, fennel seeds soothe the eyes and reduce inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh foeniculum vulgare leaves in the early morning dew and rinse them gently with distilled water.
Blend 100 grams of the fresh leaves with 200 milliliters of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil for at least 10 minutes.
Steep the blended mixture in a double boiler for 20 minutes to extract the active compounds from the foeniculum vulgare.
Strain the cooled mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass jar and discard the solids after 30 minutes of sedimentation.
Allow the infused oil to cool to room temperature before whipping it with a hand mixer for 10 minutes to create a smooth cream consistency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary creams helps with pink eye because of its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The antimicrobial compounds in rosemary, such as camphor and borneol, help to reduce bacterial growth and combat infection, soothing redness and swelling associated with conjunctivitis. Additionally, rosemary's analgesic properties ease discomfort and pain, while its antioxidant properties promote healthy tissue repair and regeneration. By applying a rosemary cream directly to the affected area, individuals can alleviate pink eye symptoms and promote healing naturally.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Rosemary's antibacterial compounds, such as rosmarinic acid, help to combat bacterial infections that can cause pink eye (conjunctivitis) by inhibiting the growth of bacteria and reducing inflammation.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in rosemary, like caffeic acid, reduce swelling and redness associated with pink eye, helping to soothe the eyes and promote healing.

Antioxidant properties: Rosemary's antioxidant properties help to protect the eyes from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to chronic eye conditions like conjunctivitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rosemary roots contain a high concentration of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing redness and inflammation associated with pink eye.
Barks: Rosemary bark has been shown to exhibit antibacterial properties, which can help combat the bacterial infections that often cause pink eye.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20g of dried rosmarinus officinalis leaves and grind them into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle within 30 minutes.
Combine the ground rosmarinus officinalis powder with 100ml of distilled water in a heat-resistant bowl and simmer for 15 minutes at 75°c to 85°c.
Strain the resulting liquid through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container discarding the solids after 10 minutes.
Mix 20g of beeswax with 40g of coconut oil and 30ml of the rosmarinus officinalis infusion in a double boiler at 65°c to 75°c for 15 minutes.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool and thicken at room temperature for 60 minutes before transferring it to glass jars for storage.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for pink eye are topical preparations derived from plants, providing soothing relief and promoting healing in cases of conjunctivitis. These creams typically contain extracts from various herbs, such as Calendula officinalis, Aloe barbadensis, Echinacea purpurea, Symphytum officinale, Melaleuca alternifolia, Euphrasia officinalis, Euphorbia peplus, Aloe vera, Plantago major, and Hypericum perforatum. If left untreated, pink eye can lead to discomfort, vision impairment, and even long-term eye damage, significantly impacting daily activities, work, and overall quality of life.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to pink eye that are treated with herbal creams are conjunctivitis, blepharitis, and styes. Herbal creams containing ingredients such as tea tree oil, aloe vera, and chamomile have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that help soothe and calm the affected areas. These creams can reduce redness, swelling, and discharge associated with these conditions, promoting faster healing and relief from discomfort.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal creams that help with pink eye is a blend of aloe vera, calendula, and tea tree oil. Aloe vera soothes and moisturizes the eye, while calendula reduces inflammation and promotes healing. Tea tree oil, with its antimicrobial properties, helps combat bacterial and viral infections that cause pink eye. Apply a thin layer of this combination to the affected area multiple times a day to promote quick healing and reduce discomfort. This natural remedy can be a gentle and effective alternative to conventional treatments.
